title: Executive Order 12525—Termination of Emergency Authority for Export Controls

flags.justification: This revokes emergency authority and rescinds a declaration of economic emergency; it reduces rather than concentrates power.
A clear, specific revocation with defined provisions preserving enforcement continuity for actions taken while the prior order was in effect.
Single coherent purpose: terminating emergency-based export control authority now that a proper statutory basis exists.
This transitions export controls from an emergency basis to a proper statutory basis rather than creating a new compliance standard.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A one-time rescission action, not an ongoing authority grant requiring reauthorization.
This restores a proper congressionally-authorized statutory basis for export controls, the opposite of bypassing legislative oversight.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
This is a corrective, contracting action retiring emergency-based authority rather than an expansive claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/8 = 0.0%. This is the resolution of the Zombie Emergency Trap chain first identified in EO 11796 and confirmed recurring in EO 11810 (both July/September 1974): Sec. 1 explicitly rescinds the declaration of economic emergency 'in view of the extension by Public Law 99-64... of the authorities contained in the Act' — meaning Congress finally passed a proper statutory reauthorization of the Export Administration Act, and the executive branch correspondingly retired the emergency-powers workaround that had substituted for it for roughly eleven years. Worth noting for the eventual writeup: the specific structural pathology identified in 1974 was not a permanent, unresolvable feature — it persisted until Congress acted, and ended when Congress did.
